summary: Megan and Chris conducted a launch readiness review for Comply v2, targeting an April 7th release. A recent Detect outage pulled two engineers off the project for four days, causing a 5-6 day slip on the HIPAA and ISO 27001 reporting engine, which has formatting issues in staging. The API is in good shape with beta customers, but customer-facing documentation for HIPAA and ISO 27001 is still in first draft and poses the biggest launch risk. They aligned on key deadlines, escalation paths, and a follow-up check-in for the following Tuesday.
actionItems: Chris Lee: Confirm Priya is fully back on Comply work starting Monday and report go/no-go status on all four frameworks to Megan by the 1st
Megan Lawson: Send intro connecting Damon to Aisha on the content team for a technical review of the HIPAA documentation today
Megan Lawson: Raise tiered API rate limiting proposal with the revenue team in Thursday's pricing sync
Chris Lee: Ensure Megan is on the distribution list for March 28th load test results and notify her same-day of any failures

keyMoments.text: Chris flags that the Detect outage pulled two engineers for four days, causing a 5-6 day slip on HIPAA and ISO 27001 report generation
keyMoments.speaker: Chris Lee

keyMoments.text: Megan confirms Detect remediation should wrap up by end of week, relieving resource pressure on the Comply team
keyMoments.speaker: Megan Lawson

keyMoments.text: Discussion of launching without HIPAA and ISO 27001 documentation on day one, which Megan flags as a support and product narrative risk
keyMoments.speaker: Megan Lawson

keyMoments.text: NorthBridge Financial requesting 100 API calls per day prompts a tiered rate limiting discussion with cost and pricing implications
keyMoments.speaker: Chris Lee
